title: NBA Daily Digest
subtitle: July 11, 2026: Summer League Heats Up, Major Signings & Shocking Altercations

items.headline: Adebayo Reportedly Punches Herro in Las Vegas Altercation!
items.summary: Heat star Bam Adebayo and former teammate Tyler Herro, recently traded to the Bucks, were involved in a physical altercation on a practice court in Las Vegas on Friday. Adebayo reportedly struck Herro after confronting him over social media comments criticizing the center.

items.headline: Wembanyama Inks Five-Year, $252M Extension with Spurs, Leaves $50M on Table
items.summary: San Antonio Spurs phenom Victor Wembanyama has officially signed a five-year, $252 million maximum rookie-scale contract extension. Reports indicate Wembanyama chose the standard 25% maximum salary, forgoing 'supermax' escalators that could have reached $303 million, to provide the Spurs with more cap flexibility for team building.

items.headline: LeBron James Free Agency Mystery Deepens: Cavs & Heat Lead Race Amid 'Done Deal' Buzz
items.summary: LeBron James' free agency continues to be the hottest topic, with his former teams, the Cleveland Cavaliers and Miami Heat, emerging as the main favorites. Insider Bill Simmons claims a deal with the Cavaliers is 'done,' while other reports suggest as many as 10 teams are in contention for the 41-year-old superstar.

items.headline: MIA Dominates MIL in Summer League Opener
Pacers Edge Cavs in Tight Contest
Celtics Win OT Thriller Against Raptors
Bulls Rookie Caleb Wilson Explodes in Summer League Debut
items.summary: Chicago Bulls' No. 4 overall pick Caleb Wilson delivered a historic Summer League debut, scoring 35 points (the most ever in a Summer League debut) along with 3 blocks and 2 steals. He shot an impressive 57% from the field, including seven 3-pointers.

items.headline: Deni Avdija: 2026 All-Star Dominates Last Season
items.summary: Israeli sensation Deni Avdija had a breakout 2025-26 season, earning his first NBA All-Star selection. Playing for the Portland Trail Blazers, he averaged 24.2 points, 6.9 rebounds, and 6.7 assists in 66 games. He also recorded a spectacular 41 points in his 2026 playoff debut.

items.headline: Nets Rookie Brown Shines in Summer League
items.summary: The Brooklyn Nets' No. 6 overall pick in the 2026 NBA Draft, Brown, led his team with 20 points and 3 assists in his Summer League debut against the Knicks. He caught fire in the second half, hitting four consecutive 3-pointers.

items.headline: Spurs Rookies Show Promise
items.summary: San Antonio's 2026 draft picks showed glimpses of potential. Tarris Reed Jr. posted a double-double with 14 points and 10 rebounds, while Ja'Kobi Gillespie contributed 12 points and 6 assists in their Summer League action.
items.statLabel: T. Reed Jr. (14 Pts, 10 Reb), J. Gillespie (12 Pts, 6 Ast)

items.headline: Four-Team Blockbuster Trade Finalized
items.summary: A major four-team trade has officially been completed, sending LaMelo Ball to the Minnesota Timberwolves, Julius Randle to the Brooklyn Nets, Naz Reid to the Charlotte Hornets, and Nic Claxton to the Chicago Bulls. This move reshapes multiple rosters heading into the new season.

items.headline: Kawhi Leonard Trade to Raptors on Hold Amid NBA Investigation
items.summary: The trade sending Kawhi Leonard back to the Toronto Raptors from the Los Angeles Clippers has been put on hold, pending the conclusion of an NBA investigation. The deal, agreed upon in June, involves Brandon Ingram, Gradey Dick, and multiple draft picks heading to the Clippers.

items.headline: Tyrese Haliburton Out for 2025-26 Season with Torn Achilles
items.summary: Indiana Pacers star Tyrese Haliburton will miss the entire 2025-26 NBA season after suffering a torn right Achilles tendon in Game 7 of the NBA Finals. This is a significant blow for the Pacers' upcoming campaign.

items.headline: Key Free Agent Signings Made Official
items.summary: Several free agent signings were officially completed on Friday. Notable moves include Ousmane Dieng signing a three-year deal with the Bucks, Jordan Miller a three-year deal with the Clippers, and Marcus Smart a two-year deal with the Rockets. Additionally, the Jazz added Jaxson Hayes, Josh Okogie, and Mo Bamba, while the Timberwolves secured Ayo Dosunmu and Jaylen Clark.
